Strings.S:
\r \r \r \r Back\r \r \r
\r \r \r

Editor
The Track Editor is a powerful tool, which you can use to create new tracks to play on and share with your friends. All of the game’s existing tracks are created with this very same tool, so there’s nothing to stop you from making tracks that are just as good as the ones in the game - or even better! The best way to learn how to use the Track Editor is just to use it and play with the features. Go ahead, it's fun!

The Track Editor is divided into two different modes: the Simple Mode and the Advanced Mode. In Simple Mode, you can create basic tracks and have limited control over the camera. Object placement and selection is also limited to the line the bike rides on. You can easily lay down the basic structure of your track in Simple Mode.

In Advanced Mode, you have more control over the camera and object placement and the selection of objects available to you is wider. You can also adjust object properties to trigger physics effects, with which you can create objects that are affected by gravity and other forces. You can also create Skill Game tracks in Advanced Mode. You can fine-tune and tweak your track in more detail in Advanced Mode.

In both modes, you can see the currently available commands listed on the bottom of the screen. You can select various items, rotate them and place them wherever you want on the track. Objects will stay where you place them (even if they are floating in the air) unless you enable physics from their properties.

Tool Summary
The track editor is fairly intuitive to use; merely paying attention to the commands listed at the bottom of the editor screen will generally help you to build the tracks you want. However, the following functions are especially useful:

Copy- You can copy the element you have selected. This saves time and effort, and can be particularly useful with the multi-select function (see below), as it enables you to quickly fill up the track. You can copy the selected object with the (Y) button.

Align to riding line- By pressing the (RIGHT STICK) button once, the current selected object is centered over the riding line. When pressing the button a second time, the object’s rotation is also reset.

Rotation snap- The (LEFT BUMPER) button rotates the object 90 degrees around the selected axis. If the selected object is at an arbitrary angle, it is first reset to its original rotation from where a consecutive (LEFT BUMPER) button pressing will snap rotate it by 90 degrees.

Multi-select- With the multi-select option you can select several objects and move them around at once. If you use the copy function, you can easily and quickly duplicate large sections of the track. You can multi-select objects by holding down the (LEFT BUMPER) button and then selecting the objects.

Glue- With the glue option you can attach multiple objects to form a single object, enabling you to create more easily manipulated, large sections of your track. You can glue (or unglue) the selected object by selecting “Glue to Object” from the object’s properties. Note: To unglue, you must use Sub-select (see below) to select the glued object from within the group.

Sub-select- With the sub-select option you can select and manipulate specific elements from a larger object you have created by gluing several objects together. You can sub-select objects by holding down the (LEFT BUMPER) button and then selecting the elements you want. It is basically the same as Multi-select.

Checkpoint jumping- During track testing within the editor, pressing the (LEFT BUMPER) button or (RIGHT BUMPER) button skips back and forth between the checkpoints for swift and easy testing of the level.

Area Complexity
There are limits to how complex your tracks can become. You'll find that these limits won't be too easily reached, but attempting to cram a vast number of physics objects into a small space, for example, would make the game run too slowly. If you reach the Area Complexity limit, you’ll have to space the track elements apart more or remove track elements.

Checkpoints
You can put a total of 30 checkpoints on the track; these include the first and last checkpoints. The last checkpoint in the sequence is always the final line the player must cross to complete the track.

Opening Rooms
In Advanced Mode, you can adjust the rooms by adding or removing them to allow additional variety in the track’s design. Rooms can be selected as any other object with the (A) button and then pressing either (X) button to delete the room or (Y) button to create a room next to the selected wall.

Editor Menu
You can access the Editor Menu by pressing the (START) button. From here you can take a test ride on the track, choose the bike for your test ride and adjust track settings. You can save the track you’ve just created, load an existing track or start a brand new track, as well as manage existing tracks. You can switch between Simple Mode and Advanced Mode from this menu. You can also access this help text - although presumably you’ve already noticed that!

Object properties: Physics
In the Track Editor, “physics” is an object-specific concept. When an object has physics turned on, that means it's attracted by gravity, momentum and other forces, such as the player's bike. Such an object can fall down or be toppled over, and its weight and momentum can affect other physics objects - again, including the player’s bike! Physics objects can be used to create fun tracks in which the player has to interact with them to create a path to proceed on, or in which the track suddenly changes when the player reaches a certain point. You can see how falling objects and other physics play out in practice by selecting the Physics On/Off function with the (BACK) button.

Hinges
A hinge is a special object that enables you to create more complex physical objects such as cars and swings. To create a working hinge: Create the hinge-object. Next you must glue objects separately to both ends of the hinge - you cannot glue the hinge itself, you must glue other objects to it. Additionally, once you’ve glued both of the objects to the hinge, you must enable physics on the objects that you wish to behave according to the physics

Triggers
A trigger is a way to have something happen on the track when the player reaches a certain point on it. When you place a trigger area on the track, you must link it to another object and select which properties of the object you wish to be engaged by that trigger. When the player rides over the trigger, these properties will be engaged.

Physics- gravity and other forces start to affect the object. Things fall down, bounce off other objects, can be pushed around by the bike, etc.

Explode/break- the object breaks apart or explodes when triggered. Note: Not all objects can be broken.

Light- a light is switched on or off.

Animation- triggers the movement of an animated object, such as the flippers. If you wish the animated object to interact with the player, in addition to “animation”, “physics” must also be selected.

Creating Skill Games
To choose the Skill Game you wish to create, select Track Settings from the Pause Menu inside the editor and from there adjust the Track Style. There are 12 different Skill Games to choose from.

Note: In all of the Skill Games, use only one checkpoint to start the track. Multiple checkpoints may cause undesired effects.


Ball Inside
Your goal is to move as far as possible inside the giant iron ball.

#1:Create object (D-PAD UP) -> Skill Game Objects -> Giant Iron Ball Inside
#2:Select the newly-created ball and press (D-PAD UP) to access its properties. Make sure both Physics and Lock to Driving line are enabled.
#3:Center the ball to the driving line and place the Checkpoint so that it points to the center of the inside of the Giant Iron Ball.


Ski Jump
Jump as far as possible. Touching the ground will mark the length of your jump.

#1:Create object (D-PAD UP) -> Skill Game Objects -> Ski-jump Lip Trigger
#2:Place the trigger objects in mid air, after a ramp. The length of the jump begins to be recorded once the bike touches the trigger.

Note:Make sure the bike is completely airborne when the trigger is activated or else the jump is considered a failure.

The bike is restricted to the Scorpion Air 125cc and no other bikes can be used.


Rings of Fire
Fly through as many Rings of Fire as possible. Each time you pass through a Ring of Fire, additional rocket fuel is added.

#1:Create object (D-PAD UP) -> Skill Game Objects -> Ring of Fire Start Trigger
#2:Place the trigger objects in mid air so that the bike will pass through it before collecting the first Ring of Fire.
#3:Create object (D-PAD UP) -> Skill Game Objects -> Ring of Fire
#4:Copy and place the Ring of Fire to create your level.

Note 1:Make sure the bike is completely airborne when the trigger is activated or else the flight is considered a failure.
Note 2:You are going need to clear out many rooms to make space for the flight.


Delivery
Move the bombs as far as you can in the delivery cart. Bigger bumps and impacts to the delivery cart will cause the bombs to explode, so plan your track accordingly.

The bike is restricted to the Hauler 600cc and no other bikes can be used.


In Flames
Try to survive as far as possible while engulfed in flames. When planning your level, remember that the faster you drive, the more at bay the fire will remain.

Note:For the driver to catch fire there needs to be fire effects on the driving line through which the bike must go. For example: Create object (D-PAD UP) -> Effects -> Fire 2 and place it on the driving line.


Target Hunt
Collect as many targets as you can without faulting.

#1:Create object (D-PAD UP) -> Skill Game Objects -> Target hunt score
#2:Place the Crash Targets around the level.


Hill Climb
Get as high as you can.

Note:Bail-out will end the track.


Ball Outside
Travel on top of the ball as far as possible without falling down from it.

#1:Create object (D-PAD UP) -> Skill Game Objects -> Giant Iron Ball Outside
#2Select the newly-created ball and press (D-PAD UP) to access its properties. Make sure both Physics and Lock to Driving line are enabled.
#3Center the ball to the driving line and place the Checkpoint so that it points to the top of the Giant Iron Ball.

The bike is restricted to the Phoenix REV 250cc and no other bikes can be used.


Height Jump
Acquire as much altitude as you can.

Note:Bail-out can be used to reach even greater heights.


Flip
Do as many flips as you can. Faulting will end the game.

Note:Time limit is 1 minute.


Wheelie
Wheelie for as long and as far as you can. Avoid letting the front wheel touch the ground.
